crime.value: Murder of ballerina Gabriela Bunsdorff during ballet performance
crime.quote: the murder of Gabriela Bunsdorff. It had happened years ago when the ballet, "Sleeping Beauty" performed

victim.value: Gabriela Bunsdorff, ballerina whose death allowed understudy to advance career
victim.quote: then Gabriela danced the death dance and the rest is history

closed_world.value: Baranovsky Ballet Company performing at Nautilus Beach Convention Center
closed_world.quote: when the ballet, "Sleeping Beauty" performed by the Baranovsky Ballet Company came to Nautilus Beach

culprit_and_motive.value: Natasha Dubcek (understudy who benefited from victim's death to advance ballet career)
culprit_and_motive.quote: you were just a dancer, an understudy, then Gabriela danced the death dance and the rest is history

resolution.value: Investigation involving forensic analysis of glass evidence from victim's dressing room
resolution.quote: looking for one that hopefully contained the pieces of the broken glass taken from Gabriela's dressing room

investigator.value: Robert Wallace, former homicide detective now reflecting on past case
investigator.quote: Robert Wallace sat in his wheelchair thinking, remembering, the crime, the suspects and the investigation

suspect_architecture.value: Seven suspects from ballet company hierarchy: Doctor Edwards, Anthony Rogers, Natasha Dubcek (understudy/beneficiary), Margaret Helms, Harry Brummel, Viktor Baranovsky (company owner), Lester Cummings. Clear means/motive/opportunity spread across company roles.
suspect_architecture.quote: Chapter Two.....Doctor Edwards Chapter Three..Anthony Rogers Chapter Four....Natasha Dubcek

red_herring.value: Romantic subplot between Wallace and Natasha appears to be misdirection from her true motive - advancing career through Gabriela's death and marriage to company owner Viktor.
red_herring.quote: marriage to Viktor gives me forty per cent of the Barnaovsky Ballet Company

clue_fairness.value: Physical evidence search in dumpster for broken glass from victim's dressing room - clue available to reader when discovered by investigator.
clue_fairness.quote: he heard the tinkling sound of glass shards hitting one another. 'Bingo!', said Wallace

social_world.value: Ballet company hierarchy with clear power structure: Viktor Baranovsky as owner, principal dancers, understudies. Economic pressure from tourist trade and city fathers wanting quick resolution.
social_world.quote: The city fathers wanted the crime solved in a hurry and he was assigned to the case

reveal_mechanic.value: Glass evidence analysis through CSI Kevin English, combined with retrospective narrative structure as disabled Wallace remembers the case years later.
reveal_mechanic.quote: What I want you to do is examine each piece of glass for fingerprints

media_and_audience.value: Murder's negative impact on tourist trade creates external pressure; merchants and city fathers frame crime as economic threat to seaside resort reputation.
media_and_audience.quote: The death of Gabriela Bunsdorff, ballerina, had an unfavorable impact on the tourist trade

investigator_wound.value: Wallace now wheelchair-bound, reflecting on past case, suggesting physical and emotional toll. Betrayal by Natasha who used him for protection while pursuing her own ambitions.
investigator_wound.quote: Robert Wallace sat in his wheelchair thinking, remembering, the crime, the suspects

_meta.title: ebook robert f clifton la danza morte 039
